metadata.description: Deploy Intune Analytics Platform - One-click deployment with Function App + ADX + Code
Base name for all resources (max 15 chars)
GitHub repository URL containing the function code
Deploy new free-tier ADX cluster? Set false if using existing cluster.
URI of existing ADX cluster (only if deployAdxCluster is false)
Name of existing ADX database (only if deployAdxCluster is false)

resources.comments: Consumption plan for Function App (free tier)
resources.name: [variables('appServicePlanName')]

resources.comments: User-assigned managed identity for deployment scripts
resources.name: [concat(parameters('baseName'), '-deploy-id')]

resources.comments: Grant Contributor role to deployment identity for Function App deployment
resources.name: [guid(resourceGroup().id, concat(parameters('baseName'), '-deploy-id'), 'Contributor')]

resources.comments: Deploy function code from GitHub via ZIP deploy
resources.name: deployFunctionCode

properties.scriptContent:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Login with managed identity
echo 'Authenticating with managed identity...'
az login --identity
az account set --subscription "$SUBSCRIPTION_ID"

# Download repo as ZIP
REPO_ZIP_URL="${REPO_URL}/archive/refs/heads/${REPO_BRANCH}.zip"
echo "Downloading from: $REPO_ZIP_URL"
curl -sL -o repo.zip "$REPO_ZIP_URL"

# Extract
unzip -q repo.zip
REPO_DIR=$(ls -d */ | head -1)
cd "$REPO_DIR"

# Create deployment package (only function files)
echo 'Creating deployment package...'
zip -rq ../deploy.zip host.json requirements.txt fn_* shared/
cd ..

# Deploy to Function App
echo 'Deploying to Function App...'
az functionapp deployment source config-zip \
  --resource-group "$RESOURCE_GROUP" \
  --name "$FUNCTION_APP_NAME" \
  --src deploy.zip \
  --timeout 300

echo 'Deployment complete!'
echo "{\"status\": \"success\"}" > $AZ_SCRIPTS_OUTPUT_PATH

resources.comments: Grant Function App ingestor access to ADX database
resources.name: [concat(variables('adxClusterName'), '/', variables('adxDatabaseName'), '/FunctionAppIngestor')]

resources.comments: ADX Schema - Creates tables, materialized views, and functions
resources.name: [concat(variables('adxClusterName'), '/', variables('adxDatabaseName'), '/CreateSchema')]

grantPermissionsCommand.value:
[concat('# Run this in Azure Cloud Shell (PowerShell)
$miId = "', reference(resourceId('Microsoft.Web/sites', variables('functionAppName')), '2022-09-01', 'Full').identity.principalId, '"
$graphId = (Get-AzADServicePrincipal -ApplicationId "00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000").Id
New-AzADServicePrincipalAppRoleAssignment -ServicePrincipalId $miId -ResourceId $graphId -AppRoleId "dc377aa6-52d8-4e23-b271-2a7ae04cedf3"
New-AzADServicePrincipalAppRoleAssignment -ServicePrincipalId $miId -ResourceId $graphId -AppRoleId "dc377aa6-52d8-4e23-b271-2a7ae6b159e6"')]

value.step2: Open Azure Cloud Shell (PowerShell) at https://shell.azure.com
value.step3: Paste and run the command to grant Graph API permissions
